title: git worktree branch arg: no git-check-ref-format validation; also boolean flag apply_changes
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

**Dimension:** Security / Structural Modularity

**Location:** src/tools/worktree.rs lines 84 (worktree_dir join), 97 (git add -b &branch), 168 (apply_changes bool), 203-208 (git commit), 231/244 (duplicate cleanup).

**Violation:** (1) branch arg from model has no validation — relies on git's own (post-2.17) flag-prefix rejection; the PathBuf at worktree_dir = git_root.join(".worktrees").join(&branch) escapes when branch contains '../'. (2) exit_worktree uses apply_changes: bool — violates the boolean-flag split rule (matches execute_bash run_in_background). (3) git commit -m runs hooks with agent privileges; a malicious .git/hooks/pre-commit in the cloned worktree runs on exit_worktree(apply_changes=true). (4) worktree remove --force is duplicated verbatim at lines 231 and 244 — DRY violation.

**Mandated Refactor:** (1) Validate branch via git2::Reference::is_valid_name or reject '..', '//', '@{', ASCII control, leading '-' or '/'. (2) Split into exit_worktree_discard() / exit_worktree_merge(). (3) Set GIT_CONFIG_COUNT/KEY/VALUE to pin core.hooksPath=/dev/null during auto-commit. (4) Extract cleanup_worktree() helper.
